access_status: Avastin requires inadequate response, contraindication, or intolerance to bevacizumab-awwb (Mvasi) OR bevacizumab-bvzr (Zirabev) before approval; biosimilars Mvasi and Zirabev are preferred
prior_auth_criteria: Must meet ALL of the following: (1) Used to treat an indication listed in Table 1 with all indication-specific criteria met; (2) Dose does not exceed 10 mg/kg every 2 weeks OR 15 mg/kg every 3 weeks; (3) For Avastin specifically, member must have inadequate response, contraindication, or intolerance to bevacizumab-awwb (Mvasi) OR bevacizumab-bvzr (Zirabev) with documentation submitted. Step therapy exception applies if a prior health plan paid for Avastin within the past 90 days (documentation required). Initial approval duration: 180 days. Continuation requires previous approval by Florida Blue or another health plan in past 2 years OR member previously met all indication-specific criteria, plus dose limits; continuation approval duration: 1 year.
covered_indications: Ampullary cancer - unresectable, advanced, or metastatic intestinal type disease in combination with FOLFOX, CapeOX, FOLFIRINOX, capecitabine, fluorouracil/leucovorin, or FOLFIRI

Cervical cancer - persistent, recurrent, or metastatic disease in combination with paclitaxel/cisplatin, paclitaxel/carboplatin, or paclitaxel/topotecan; or with chemotherapy and pembrolizumab; or with chemotherapy and atezolizumab; or as single agent second-line or subsequent therapy

CNS cancer - recurrent or progressive glioblastoma, high grade glioma, intracranial/spinal ependymoma, meningiomas, WHO Grade 3 or 4 IDH-mutant Astrocytoma, WHO Grade 3 Oligodendroglioma as single agent; recurrent/progressive disease in combination with carmustine, lomustine, or temozolomide (with or without irinotecan) for glioblastoma, high grade glioma, medulloblastoma, WHO Grade 3 or 4 IDH-mutant Astrocytoma, WHO Grade 3 Oligodendroglioma; recurrent/progressive medulloblastoma with temozolomide and irinotecan; symptoms of radiation necrosis, poorly controlled vasogenic edema or mass effect; Neurofibromatosis type 2 vestibular schwannomas as single agent; pediatric diffuse high-grade gliomas or pediatric medulloblastoma

Colon and rectal cancer (including anal adenocarcinoma and appendiceal adenocarcinoma) - metastatic, unresectable, advanced, or medically inoperable disease in combination with FOLFOX, FOLFIRI, CapeOX, FOLFIRINOX, irinotecan, irinotecan and oxaliplatin, capecitabine, or 5-FU/LV; or as subsequent therapy with trifluridine/tipiracil (Lonsurf) after progression on fluoropyrimidine-, oxaliplatin-, and irinotecan-based therapy

Hepatocellular carcinoma - unresectable disease not candidate for transplant, metastatic disease, inoperable due to performance status or comorbidities, or at high risk of recurrence; used in combination with atezolizumab
Hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia - received red blood cell transfusions for HHT-associated bleeding; or liver vascular malformations with symptomatic high-output cardiac failure

Kidney cancer - relapsed or stage IV disease in combination with interferon alfa-2b; or with everolimus in non-clear cell histology; or with erlotinib in advanced papillary renal cell carcinoma including HLRCC

Mesothelioma - pleural mesothelioma with pemetrexed and cisplatin or carboplatin every 3 weeks for 6 cycles followed by single-agent maintenance; peritoneal, pericardial, and tunica vaginalis testis mesothelioma with pemetrexed and cisplatin or carboplatin for 6 cycles followed by single-agent maintenance; or with atezolizumab as subsequent therapy if not previously treated with immune checkpoint inhibitors

Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) - non-squamous, advanced, recurrent, or metastatic disease in combination with pemetrexed/cisplatin, pemetrexed/carboplatin, paclitaxel/carboplatin, paclitaxel/carboplatin/atezolizumab, nab-paclitaxel/carboplatin (with documented hypersensitivity to conventional paclitaxel or docetaxel), atezolizumab maintenance, pemetrexed maintenance, erlotinib, or single agent maintenance

Ovarian cancer (including epithelial, fallopian tube, primary peritoneal, and less common histologies) - recurrent or persistent disease as single agent or in combination with cyclophosphamide, cyclophosphamide/pembrolizumab, gemcitabine, gemcitabine/carboplatin, liposomal doxorubicin/carboplatin, liposomal doxorubicin, mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx, paclitaxel, paclitaxel/carboplatin, or topotecan; or with paclitaxel/carboplatin; or with docetaxel/carboplatin; or with docetaxel/oxaliplatin; or single agent maintenance; or with olaparib or niraparib for maintenance after complete or partial remission with primary treatment containing bevacizumab
Radiation necrosis of the brain - when member is symptomatic
Small bowel adenocarcinoma - advanced or metastatic disease in combination with FOLFOX, FOLFIRI, CapeOX, FOLFIRINOX, capecitabine, or 5-FU/LV
Soft-tissue sarcoma - single agent for angiosarcoma; or in combination with temozolomide for solitary fibrous tumor
Uterine neoplasm - single agent in members progressed on prior cytotoxic chemotherapy; or with carboplatin and paclitaxel; or single agent maintenance
Vaginal cancer - single agent or with carboplatin/paclitaxel, cisplatin/paclitaxel, pembrolizumab/paclitaxel/carboplatin, pembrolizumab/paclitaxel/cisplatin, or topotecan/paclitaxel
Vulvar cancer - advanced, unresectable, recurrent or metastatic disease in combination with paclitaxel and cisplatin or carboplatin
Other FDA-approved indications consistent with prescribing information or NCCN Drugs and Biologics Compendium Category 1 or 2A recommendations
site_of_care: Intravenous (IV) infusion; first infusion over 90 minutes, second infusion over 60 minutes if tolerated, subsequent infusions over 30 minutes if second infusion tolerated; do not administer as IV bolus or push
quantity_limits: Dose must not exceed 10 mg/kg every 2 weeks OR 15 mg/kg every 3 weeks.

Specific FDA-approved dosing varies by indication (e.g., metastatic colorectal cancer: 5-10 mg/kg every 2 weeks or 7.5 mg/kg every 3 weeks; NSCLC: 15 mg/kg every 3 weeks; recurrent glioblastoma: 10 mg/kg every 2 weeks; metastatic RCC: 10 mg/kg every 2 weeks; cervical cancer: 15 mg/kg every 3 weeks; ovarian cancer stage III/IV post-surgery: 15 mg/kg every 3 weeks up to 22 cycles total; hepatocellular carcinoma: 15 mg/kg every 3 weeks).
notes: Policy covers bevacizumab (Avastin) and biosimilars bevacizumab-awwb (Mvasi), bevacizumab-bvzr (Zirabev), bevacizumab-maly (Alymsys), bevacizumab-nwgd (Jobevne), bevacizumab-tnjn (Avzivi), and bevacizumab-adcd (Vegzelma). Mvasi and Zirabev are the preferred (step therapy) agents; Avastin, Alymsys, Avzivi, Jobevne, and Vegzelma require documented inadequate response, contraindication, or intolerance to Mvasi or Zirabev. Step therapy exemption applies if a prior health plan paid for Avastin within the past 90 days (documentation required). Ophthalmic indications are excluded and covered under separate guideline MCG #09-J1000-78. Federal Employee Program (FEP) and State Account Organization (SAO) follow their own guidelines. Medicare Advantage: no NCD/LCD found at time of last revision for oncology indications. Florida law 627.42393 step therapy exemption process available. No recommended dose reductions; discontinuation required for serious adverse events including GI perforation, serious hemorrhage, severe arterial thromboembolic events, life-threatening VTE, hypertensive crisis, PRES, nephrotic syndrome, CHF, wound dehiscence. Temporary suspension required pre-elective surgery (at least 4 weeks) and for uncontrolled hypertension, moderate-to-severe proteinuria, severe infusion reactions.
